title: sonchus arvensis subsp. maritimus
intro: Sonchus arvensis subsp. maritimus, also known as sea sow thistle or sea thistle, is a subspecies of the annual thistle plant, native to coastal areas and often found growing in sandy soils and on beaches. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Sonchus arvensis subsp. maritimus include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as providing antioxidant and protective effects for the liver and kidneys. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by drying and using them in teas, tinctures, and salves.
